patterns.name: Two Pointers — Convergent
applies_when: input is sorted or has monotonic property
searching for pair satisfying a constraint
need to eliminate candidates from extremes
avoid_when: input is unsorted and sorting would lose information
need to find all pairs, not just one
constraint involves more than two elements without reduction
patterns.constraints: requires sorted input or monotonic property

patterns.name: Two Pointers — Same Direction
applies_when: slow pointer marks write position, fast scans
avoid_when: need stable partition with equal-key ordering
removal criteria depend on future elements
patterns.constraints: in-place modification required

patterns.name: Sliding Window — Fixed Size
applies_when: window size is given or determined
need aggregate (sum, max, count) over fixed-length subarray
can compute next window by adding right and removing left
avoid_when: window size varies based on content
patterns.constraints: subarray must be contiguous

patterns.name: Sliding Window — Variable Size
applies_when: find longest/shortest contiguous subarray meeting a condition
window expands to explore, contracts to satisfy constraint
avoid_when: constraint requires global context, not local window
elements can be negative and constraint involves sums
patterns.constraints: elements must be non-negative for sum-based constraints; use prefix-sum + hashmap for negative values

patterns.name: Binary Search
applies_when: searching for exact value or boundary
answer has monotonic property (all true then all false)
avoid_when: input is unsorted and cannot be sorted
need to find all occurrences, not just one
search space is not monotonically partitioned
patterns.constraints: requires sorted or monotonic structure

patterns.name: Prefix Sum
applies_when: multiple range sum queries on static array
need O(1) subarray sum after O(n) precomputation
subarray sum equals target (with hashmap for negative values)
avoid_when: array is frequently modified between queries
patterns.constraints: static array; for dynamic updates use segment tree or BIT

patterns.name: Kadane's Algorithm
applies_when: local decision: extend current subarray or start new
avoid_when: need the actual subarray indices (need slight modification)
non-contiguous selection allowed (use DP or greedy)
patterns.constraints: contiguous subarray only

patterns.name: Dutch National Flag
applies_when: partition array into exactly three groups
elements have three categories (low, mid, high)
must be in-place and single pass
patterns.constraints: exactly three categories

patterns.name: Merge Intervals
applies_when: input is a collection of intervals that may overlap
need to merge, insert, or find gaps between intervals
avoid_when: need dynamic interval updates (use interval tree)
patterns.constraints: sort by start coordinate first

patterns.name: Cyclic Sort
applies_when: values are in range [1, n] or [0, n-1]
find missing, duplicate, or first missing positive
O(1) space required for finding missing/duplicate
avoid_when: values are not in a known bounded range
patterns.constraints: values must be in range [1, n] or similar bounded range

patterns.name: In-Place Rotation (Reversal)
applies_when: rotate array by k positions in-place
avoid_when: k is 0 or equals array length
patterns.constraints: in-place required

patterns.name: Quick Select (Kth Element)
avoid_when: worst-case O(n) needed (use median of medians)
k is close to n (just sort)
need top-k elements in sorted order
patterns.constraints: modifies input array; use random pivot for expected O(n)

patterns.name: Binary Search on Answer
applies_when: minimize the maximum or maximize the minimum of some metric
answer space is monotonic — if D is feasible then D-1 is also feasible
feasibility for a given answer can be checked efficiently (O(N) or O(N log N))
avoid_when: exact answer can be computed directly without search

complexity.time: O(N log D * f(N)) where f(N) is feasibility check cost

patterns.constraints: requires monotonic answer space — all values below threshold are feasible (or infeasible), all above are not

patterns.name: Meet in the Middle
applies_when: N is moderate (20-40) making 2^N too slow but 2^(N/2) feasible
item values/weights too large for pseudo-polynomial DP
problem involves selecting a subset to optimize a metric
two halves can be enumerated independently then combined
avoid_when: N is small enough for full enumeration (N <= 20)
values are small enough for standard DP (W <= 10^6)
subsets from two halves cannot be meaningfully combined
patterns.constraints: split input into two halves of ~N/2; enumerate all subsets of each half; combine with sorting + binary search or two pointers

patterns.name: Greedy
applies_when: greedy choice property: local optimum leads to global optimum
activity selection, fractional knapsack, Huffman coding
avoid_when: constraints carry forward across decisions (need DP)
greedy leads to suboptimal: 0/1 knapsack, shortest path with negative edges
problem has coupled choices where taking one item affects later options
patterns.constraints: must prove greedy choice property holds; common proof: exchange argument

patterns.name: Linear Scan
applies_when: data is unsorted and one-time lookup needed
searching for first element satisfying a predicate
avoid_when: repeated queries on same dataset (build hashmap or sort first)
large dataset where O(n) per query is too slow
patterns.constraints: simplest search; no preprocessing needed

patterns.name: Counting Sort
applies_when: integer keys in small bounded range k where k is O(n)
avoid_when: k > n log n (comparison sort is better)
patterns.constraints: k = range of input values; only for integers in known range

patterns.name: Radix Sort
applies_when: range too large for counting sort but digits are bounded
need stable O(d*(n+k)) sort where d=digits, k=base
avoid_when: d * (n+k) > n log n (comparison sort is faster)
keys are floating-point or complex objects
patterns.constraints: d = number of digits, k = radix (base); uses stable counting sort as subroutine per digit; LSD (least significant digit first) is most common

patterns.name: Bitmask Enumeration
patterns.constraints: n must be small; each subset accessed in O(1) via bitmask

patterns.name: Sqrt Decomposition / Mo's Algorithm
applies_when: offline range queries where add/remove is O(1)
problems where segment tree merge is hard (count distinct, mode)
simpler alternative to persistent data structures
avoid_when: segment tree or BIT solves it in O(n log n + Q log n)
patterns.constraints: Mo's algorithm: sort queries by (block of left endpoint, right endpoint); Hilbert curve ordering reduces constant by ~2x

patterns.name: Bit Manipulation
applies_when: find single missing/duplicate number in O(1) space via XOR
isolate/clear lowest set bit, count set bits
patterns.constraints: XOR: a^a=0, a^0=a; Brian Kernighan popcount: O(k) where k=set bits; lowest set bit: n&(-n)

patterns.name: Sweep Line
applies_when: interval scheduling, counting overlaps at any point
avoid_when: simple interval merge suffices (greedy sort-and-merge is simpler)
online/streaming events arriving out of order
patterns.constraints: sort events by x-coordinate; maintain active set in balanced BST; Bentley-Ottmann: O((n+k) log n) for k intersections

patterns.name: Sort-First-Then-Process
applies_when: need ordered data for subsequent operations
enabling binary search or two-pointer techniques
patterns.constraints: dominated by the sort step; subsequent processing is typically O(n)

patterns.name: Brute Force Nested Loop
patterns.constraints: baseline approach; always correct but often too slow

patterns.name: Sorted Array + Binary Search
patterns.constraints: keep array sorted; use bisect/lower_bound for O(log n) lookups

patterns.name: Sorted Array with Binary Insert
applies_when: alternative to heap for median tracking on small n
avoid_when: large n with frequent inserts (O(n) shifting)
use heap or balanced BST instead

complexity.time: O(n) per insert, O(log n) search

patterns.constraints: insertion requires shifting elements; only practical for small n

patterns.name: Extra Array Copy
applies_when: simpler logic with separate output array
avoid_when: array is very large and memory is tight
patterns.constraints: trade space for simplicity

patterns.name: Boolean/Visited Array
applies_when: elements are small non-negative integers in bounded range
marking visited nodes in graph traversal
avoid_when: elements are large or sparse (use hashset)
patterns.constraints: faster than hashset when key range is small and dense

patterns.name: Sort Then Scan
applies_when: detect duplicates by sorting then checking neighbors
patterns.constraints: modifies array order; use copy if original order needed

patterns.name: Sort Then Group
applies_when: need groups by value in sorted order
avoid_when: hashmap grouping is O(n) and sufficient
patterns.constraints: sort by grouping key; then single pass to collect groups

patterns.name: Sort Then Merge (Set Operations)
avoid_when: hashset is faster for single operation
arrays already sorted (just use two pointers)

complexity.time: O(n log n + m log m)

patterns.constraints: sort both, then two-pointer merge

patterns.name: Two Pointers on Sorted Arrays
avoid_when: arrays not sorted (sort first or use hashset)
patterns.constraints: both inputs must be sorted

patterns.name: Divide and Conquer Max Subarray
applies_when: parallel computing where D&C parallelizes well
patterns.constraints: strictly worse than Kadane's for basic 1D problem; T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n)

patterns.name: Convex Hull (Andrew's Monotone Chain)
applies_when: find convex hull of 2D point set
farthest pair of points (rotating calipers on hull)
geometric optimization (max area triangle, min enclosing shape)
avoid_when: only need closest pair (simpler algorithms exist)
patterns.constraints: Andrew's monotone chain: sort by (x,y), build upper+lower hull separately; asymptotically optimal (proven lower bound)

patterns.name: Closest Pair of Points
applies_when: find minimum Euclidean distance between N points in 2D
avoid_when: need nearest neighbor for many query points (use KD-tree)
patterns.constraints: divide and conquer: split by x, recurse, merge checking strip of width 2d; each point checks at most 7 neighbors in strip

patterns.name: Sieve of Eratosthenes
applies_when: generate all primes up to N (~10^7 practical limit)
precompute smallest prime factor for factorization
bulk prime checking for many numbers
avoid_when: primes in range [L,R] with R > 10^7 (use segmented sieve)
memory constrained for very large N
patterns.constraints: mark multiples starting from 2; only sieve up to sqrt(N); skip even numbers to halve work

patterns.name: Inclusion-Exclusion Principle
applies_when: count elements in union of overlapping sets
count integers coprime to set of primes
avoid_when: direct counting formula or DP exists

complexity.time: O(2^N * f) where N=sets, f=intersection cost

patterns.constraints: |A∪B∪C| = |A|+|B|+|C| - |A∩B| - |A∩C| - |B∩C| + |A∩B∩C|; alternate sign with subset size

patterns.name: KMP String Matching
applies_when: find all occurrences of pattern in text
avoid_when: pattern is very short (brute force is fine)
patterns.constraints: exact matching only

patterns.name: Rabin-Karp Rolling Hash
patterns.constraints: hash collisions require verification

patterns.name: Two Pointers on String
avoid_when: string is immutable and cannot be modified
patterns.constraints: for mutation, convert to char array first

patterns.name: Expand Around Center
applies_when: need all palindromes centered at each position
patterns.constraints: check both odd and even length centers

patterns.name: StringBuilder Accumulation
applies_when: building result string character by character
many string concatenations in a loop
avoid_when: functional approach with joinToString is cleaner
patterns.constraints: use StringBuilder or equivalent mutable buffer

patterns.name: Brute Force String Matching
patterns.constraints: simplest string search; no preprocessing

patterns.name: Manacher's Algorithm
applies_when: find longest palindromic substring in O(n)
compute palindrome radius at every position
avoid_when: need all distinct palindromic substrings (eertree is better)
patterns.constraints: transform string with separators for even-length palindromes; each position visited at most twice

patterns.name: Aho-Corasick
applies_when: search for multiple fixed patterns simultaneously
avoid_when: patterns change frequently (automaton must be rebuilt)
patterns are regexes not fixed strings

complexity.time: O(m*k + n + z) where m=total pattern length, k=alphabet, z=matches

patterns.constraints: build failure links like KMP but on trie; automaton construction is one-time cost

patterns.name: DP Palindromic (Subsequence/Substring)
avoid_when: longest palindromic substring only (Manacher's is O(n))
string very long and O(n^2) too slow
patterns.constraints: dp[i][j] = LPS of s[i..j]; alternative: LPS = LCS(s, reverse(s))

patterns.name: Join-to-String (Efficient Concatenation)
patterns.constraints: collect parts in list/array, join once at end

patterns.name: Z-Function
applies_when: string compression / smallest repeating unit detection
patterns.constraints: z[i] = length of longest substring starting at i matching a prefix of s; often cleaner to implement than KMP

patterns.name: Rolling Hash (Polynomial Hashing)
applies_when: comparing many substrings for equality in O(1)
avoid_when: adversarial inputs (use double hashing with large primes)
patterns.constraints: polynomial hash: h(s) = sum(s[i] * p^i) mod m; use double hashing to reduce collision probability; avoid mod 2^64 (vulnerable to anti-hash attacks)

patterns.name: Suffix Automaton / Suffix Tree
applies_when: multiple substring queries on same text
longest repeated substring, longest common substring
avoid_when: memory constrained (suffix trees have large constants ~20N bytes)
only LCP queries needed (suffix array is more practical)
patterns.constraints: suffix automaton is more memory-efficient than suffix tree; suffix array + LCP array is 4-8x less memory for most applications

patterns.name: Single-Pass Complement Lookup
applies_when: find pair of elements satisfying arithmetic constraint
complement = target - current can be precomputed
single pass needed with O(1) lookup
avoid_when: input is sorted (two pointers is O(1) space)
need all pairs, not just first
constraint is not decomposable into complement
patterns.constraints: space for hash map

patterns.name: Frequency Aggregation
applies_when: need to count occurrences of each element
decisions based on frequency (top-k, mode, threshold)
avoid_when: elements are in small fixed range (use array instead)
only need presence check, not count
patterns.constraints: k = number of distinct elements

patterns.name: Grouping by Key
applies_when: group elements sharing a computed property
canonical form can represent equivalence classes
e.g., sorted character sequence groups anagrams
avoid_when: groups are predefined and small (use array of lists)
grouping key is expensive to compute
patterns.constraints: key function must be consistent for equivalent items

patterns.name: Index / Value Mapping
applies_when: need to look up index/position of previously seen element
mapping values to their most recent index
tracking element positions for constraint checking
avoid_when: values are in small range (use array)
patterns.constraints: handles duplicate values by overwriting (last seen) or storing list

patterns.name: Cache / Memoization Lookup
applies_when: function called with same inputs repeatedly
patterns.constraints: key must fully represent input state

patterns.name: Prefix Sum with HashMap
applies_when: find subarrays summing to target k
need count of all qualifying subarrays
avoid_when: all values are positive (sliding window works)
need actual subarray boundaries, not just count
patterns.constraints: map stores prefix_sum -> count of occurrences

patterns.name: Sliding Window with Map/Set
applies_when: sliding window needs to track element frequencies
constraint involves distinct count or frequency match
e.g., at most K distinct, minimum window containing all
avoid_when: simple sum/count constraint (plain window is enough)
character set is small (use array instead of map)
patterns.constraints: update map on both expand and shrink

patterns.name: Counting Array
applies_when: keys are non-negative integers in small range
faster than hashmap for bounded integer keys
avoid_when: keys are large, sparse, or non-integer
range is unknown at compile time
patterns.constraints: direct indexed array; k must be bounded and reasonable

patterns.name: Trie-Based Frequency Counting
applies_when: counting word frequencies with prefix-based queries needed
avoid_when: no prefix queries needed (hashmap is simpler)

complexity.time: O(m) per word where m = word length

patterns.constraints: combines trie structure with frequency counters at nodes

patterns.name: Set Membership Testing
avoid_when: elements are in small range (use boolean array)
patterns.constraints: elements must be hashable

patterns.name: Consecutive Sequence via Set
applies_when: find longest consecutive sequence in unsorted array
O(n) time required (sorting is O(n log n))
check if x-1 not in set to find sequence starts
avoid_when: need the actual sequence elements in order
patterns.constraints: only start expansion from sequence boundaries

patterns.name: Set Algebra (Union/Intersect/Diff)
applies_when: find elements in one but not another
patterns.constraints: elements must be hashable

patterns.name: Bloom Filter (Probabilistic Membership)
applies_when: space is critical and false positives are acceptable
avoid_when: need to delete elements (use cuckoo filter)

complexity.time: O(k) per query where k = hash functions

patterns.constraints: no false negatives; FPR tunable via k and m; no deletion without counting variant

patterns.name: Cuckoo Filter
applies_when: need deletion support without extra space
fast lookups with good cache locality
avoid_when: need union/intersection of filters (Bloom supports bitwise ops)
insertion must never fail (cuckoo fails at ~95% load)
patterns.constraints: stores fingerprints in 2 candidate buckets; deletion supported if item was previously inserted

patterns.name: Fast & Slow Pointers (Floyd's)
applies_when: detect cycle in linked list or sequence
find middle node without knowing length
avoid_when: need to find all cycles (DFS/BFS is better)
patterns.constraints: fast moves 2 steps, slow 1 step per iteration

patterns.name: Linked List Reversal
avoid_when: need random access to reversed elements (convert to array)
patterns.constraints: maintain prev, curr, next pointers

patterns.name: Merge Sorted Lists
applies_when: merge two or more sorted linked lists
merge step of merge sort on linked list
avoid_when: need merge of many lists (use heap)
patterns.constraints: use dummy/sentinel node to simplify head handling

patterns.name: Dummy / Sentinel Node
applies_when: deletion or insertion at head needs special handling
building a new list from scratch
patterns.constraints: return dummy.next as new head

patterns.name: Nth Node from End
applies_when: find or remove nth node from end
need element at known distance from tail
avoid_when: can afford two passes (get length first)
patterns.constraints: n must be valid (1 <= n <= list length)

patterns.name: List Partition
applies_when: partition list around a pivot value
patterns.constraints: use two dummy heads for sublists

patterns.name: Stack-Based Reversal
applies_when: need to reverse linked list with simple logic
avoid_when: O(1) space required (use in-place iterative reversal)
patterns.constraints: push all nodes to stack, pop to build reversed list

patterns.name: Two-Pass Length-First
applies_when: need to find nth from end or middle
patterns.constraints: first pass counts length, second pass goes to target position

patterns.name: Doubly Linked List History (Undo/Redo)
patterns.constraints: current pointer moves forward on action, backward on undo; new action truncates future

patterns.name: Balanced Delimiters
avoid_when: only one type of bracket (use counter)
need to count nesting depth only
patterns.constraints: stack must be empty at end for valid input

patterns.name: Monotonic Stack
applies_when: elements popped when invariant violated give answers
avoid_when: need random access to stack elements
problem does not involve relative ordering comparisons
patterns.constraints: each element pushed and popped at most once

patterns.name: Expression Evaluation
avoid_when: expression is already in postfix form
patterns.constraints: handle precedence: *, / before +, -

patterns.name: Undo / History Tracking
avoid_when: need redo as well (use two stacks)
patterns.constraints: stack stores states or actions

patterns.name: Min/Max Stack
applies_when: need O(1) min/max in addition to O(1) push/pop
sliding minimum/maximum over LIFO access pattern
patterns.constraints: extra stack or tuple encoding

patterns.name: DFS with Explicit Stack
applies_when: DFS on deep structures (avoid stack overflow)
avoid_when: recursion depth is bounded and manageable
patterns.constraints: push children in reverse for left-to-right order

patterns.name: Counter for Single Delimiter Type
applies_when: only one type of bracket to validate
avoid_when: need to track positions of mismatches
patterns.constraints: increment on open, decrement on close; invalid if counter goes negative or ends non-zero

patterns.name: Recursive Descent Parser
applies_when: hand-written parser that's easy to extend
avoid_when: simpler shunting-yard suffices for basic arithmetic
patterns.constraints: each non-terminal becomes a function; precedence encoded by call depth; additive calls multiplicative calls primary

patterns.name: Recursive DFS
applies_when: tree/graph traversal where recursion depth is manageable
patterns.constraints: limited by call stack depth; Python default ~1000 frames

patterns.name: Monotonic Deque
applies_when: maintaining monotonic order for O(1) access to extremum in window
avoid_when: fixed window aggregate other than min/max (use prefix sum)

complexity.space: O(k) where k is window size

patterns.constraints: front of deque is always the answer; remove from back when new element is better; remove from front when out of window

patterns.name: BFS Traversal
applies_when: find shortest path in unweighted graph/grid
explore all nodes at distance k before k+1
avoid_when: need all paths, not just shortest
memory constrained and graph is wide (DFS uses less)
patterns.constraints: use visited set to avoid revisiting

patterns.name: Multi-Source BFS
applies_when: compute distance from nearest source for all cells
rotten oranges / nearest gate type problems
patterns.constraints: enqueue all sources at distance 0 initially

patterns.name: Task Scheduling
applies_when: schedule tasks with cooldown between same-type tasks
minimize total time or idle slots
patterns.constraints: use max-heap for greedy selection + queue for cooldown; when answer is max achievable min distance, closed-form from max frequency may be O(N) vs O(N log N) binary search

patterns.name: Sliding Window Maximum (Monotonic Deque)
applies_when: find maximum/minimum in every window of size k
need O(n) total time for all windows
maintain ordered candidates as window slides
avoid_when: need more than max/min (use sorted structure)
patterns.constraints: each element enters and exits deque at most once

patterns.name: 0-1 BFS (Deque-Based)
applies_when: shortest path with only 0 and 1 weights
patterns.constraints: 0-weight edges: push to front; 1-weight: push to back

patterns.name: Heap-Based Sliding Window
applies_when: sliding window max/min when deque is hard to implement
avoid_when: simple min/max (monotonic deque is O(n) vs O(n log n))
exact window boundary tracking is complex with lazy deletion
patterns.constraints: heap with lazy deletion: mark expired elements, ignore them when they surface at top

patterns.name: Greedy Formula (Task Scheduling)
applies_when: task scheduling with deadlines and penalties
priority computed by formula (e.g., processing time / weight)
patterns.constraints: sort by computed priority; often provable via exchange argument

patterns.name: Top-K Elements
applies_when: find k largest or smallest elements
streaming data, k is much smaller than n
avoid_when: k is close to n (just sort)
need exact kth element only (use quickselect)
patterns.constraints: for k largest, use min-heap of size k

patterns.name: Merge K Sorted Sequences
applies_when: merge k sorted lists/arrays into one
patterns.constraints: heap stores one element from each sequence

patterns.name: Two Heaps (Median Maintenance)
applies_when: balance two halves of sorted data
avoid_when: need other percentiles (use more complex structure)

complexity.time: O(log n) per insert, O(1) median

patterns.constraints: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half, balance sizes

patterns.name: Dijkstra's Shortest Path
applies_when: shortest path in weighted graph with non-negative edges
avoid_when: graph has negative edge weights (use Bellman-Ford)
patterns.constraints: no negative edge weights

patterns.name: Divide and Conquer Merge (k-way)
applies_when: merge k sorted lists when heap implementation is complex
avoid_when: k-way heap merge is simpler and has same complexity
patterns.constraints: pair up lists, merge pairs, repeat; log k rounds of O(n) work

patterns.name: DFS Inorder Traversal
applies_when: get BST elements in sorted order
patterns.constraints: h = height; O(log n) balanced, O(n) skewed

patterns.name: DFS Preorder Traversal
avoid_when: need sorted order from BST (use inorder)
patterns.constraints: h = height of tree

patterns.name: DFS Postorder Traversal
applies_when: compute value from children (height, size, sum)
patterns.constraints: natural for bottom-up aggregation

patterns.name: BFS Level-Order Traversal
applies_when: process nodes by level (zigzag, right view, averages)
find minimum depth (first leaf found = answer)
avoid_when: need path from root to node (use DFS)
memory constrained (DFS uses O(h) vs O(w))
patterns.constraints: w = max width of tree; up to n/2 at bottom level

patterns.name: BST Operations
avoid_when: data is static (sorted array + binary search is simpler)
patterns.constraints: balanced BST gives O(log n); skewed gives O(n)

patterns.name: Lowest Common Ancestor
applies_when: find lowest common ancestor of two nodes
in BST: compare values to find split point
in binary tree: recursive search returning found nodes
avoid_when: need LCA queries for many pairs (use binary lifting)
patterns.constraints: both nodes must exist in tree

patterns.name: Tree Construction from Traversals
avoid_when: only one traversal is given (ambiguous without inorder)
patterns.constraints: use hashmap for O(1) inorder index lookup

patterns.name: Path Sum Accumulation
applies_when: check if root-to-leaf path sums to target
find all root-to-leaf paths with target sum
avoid_when: path does not need to be root-to-leaf (use different formulation)
patterns.constraints: pass accumulated sum as parameter; check at leaves

patterns.name: Tree Depth / Height Computation
applies_when: compute height or depth of tree
avoid_when: need minimum depth (BFS is more efficient)
patterns.constraints: height = max(left, right) + 1

patterns.name: Tree Serialization / Deserialization
applies_when: serialize tree for storage or transmission
compare tree structures via string comparison
patterns.constraints: null markers needed to distinguish structure

patterns.name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
avoid_when: very long strings with no shared prefixes

complexity.time: O(m) per operation, m = word length

patterns.constraints: each node has children map/array

patterns.name: Segment Tree
applies_when: dynamic array with frequent queries and updates
patterns.constraints: 4n space for array-based implementation

patterns.name: Fenwick Tree (BIT)
applies_when: prefix sum queries with point updates
avoid_when: need range updates (use lazy segment tree)
patterns.constraints: 1-indexed; supports prefix sum and point update

patterns.name: Binary Lifting (LCA + k-th Ancestor)
applies_when: online LCA queries on static tree
path aggregate queries (max edge, sum on path)
avoid_when: need O(1) per query (use Euler tour + sparse table)
offline batch queries (Tarjan's is O(n + Q))

complexity.time: O(n log n) preprocess, O(log n) per query

patterns.constraints: build up[v][k] = 2^k-th ancestor of v; LCA: lift deeper node, then lift both until they meet

patterns.name: Euler Tour + RMQ (LCA)
applies_when: many LCA queries where O(1) per query matters
static tree with no structural changes
avoid_when: also need k-th ancestor (binary lifting is more natural)

complexity.time: O(n log n) preprocess (sparse table), O(1) per query

patterns.constraints: Euler tour produces 2n-1 entries; LCA(u,v) = RMQ on depth between first[u] and first[v]; Farach-Colton-Bender achieves O(n) preprocess, O(1) query

patterns.name: Interval Tree
applies_when: find all intervals overlapping a query interval/point
avoid_when: only need range-sum/range-min on points (segment tree)

complexity.time: O(log n) insert/delete/query-one, O(k log n) query-all

patterns.constraints: augmented BST storing intervals; each node stores max endpoint in subtree for pruning

patterns.name: Prefix Sum on Tree
applies_when: subtree sum/count queries after Euler tour flattening
path sum using LCA: sum(root,u) + sum(root,v) - 2*sum(root,lca)
avoid_when: need point updates (use BIT/segment tree on Euler tour)
patterns.constraints: flatten tree via Euler tour; subtree of v = contiguous range [in[v], out[v]]

patterns.name: TreeMap / Ordered Map
avoid_when: only need lookup/insert/delete without ordering (hashmap is O(1))
patterns.constraints: backed by Red-Black tree (Java TreeMap, C++ std::map); always use member lower_bound in C++ (O(log n) vs free function O(n))

patterns.name: Morris Traversal
avoid_when: tree is read-only/immutable (Morris temporarily modifies tree)
simple recursive traversal is acceptable (O(h) space)
patterns.constraints: temporarily creates threads (right pointers to in-order successor); restores tree structure after traversal

patterns.name: Sparse Table
applies_when: immutable array with many range min/max/gcd queries
need O(1) per query (faster than segment tree's O(log n))
avoid_when: array values change between queries (use segment tree)

complexity.time: O(N log N) build, O(1) query for idempotent ops

patterns.constraints: precompute answers for all power-of-2-length ranges; query overlaps two precomputed ranges; only works for idempotent operations (min, max, gcd, OR)

patterns.name: Treap (Tree + Heap)
applies_when: implicit treap for sequence operations (insert/delete at position, range reverse, range queries)
avoid_when: need guaranteed worst-case O(log N) (use AVL/Red-Black)
patterns.constraints: each node has key + random priority; BST property on keys, heap property on priorities; split and merge are the fundamental operations

patterns.name: BFS Shortest Path (Unweighted)
avoid_when: need all shortest paths (modify BFS to continue)
patterns.constraints: mark visited when enqueuing, not when dequeuing

patterns.name: DFS Graph Traversal
patterns.constraints: use visited set; three-color for directed cycle detection

patterns.name: Topological Sort
avoid_when: graph has cycles (no valid topological order)
patterns.constraints: only valid for DAGs; Kahn's detects cycles

patterns.name: Union-Find (Disjoint Set)
applies_when: dynamic connectivity: are two nodes connected?
count connected components as edges are added
patterns.constraints: path compression + union by rank for near-O(1)

patterns.name: Flood Fill
avoid_when: need to find disconnected components efficiently (use union-find)
patterns.constraints: mark visited to avoid infinite loops

patterns.name: Bellman-Ford
applies_when: shortest path with negative edge weights
patterns.constraints: V-1 relaxation rounds; Vth round detects negative cycle

patterns.name: SPFA (Shortest Path Faster Algorithm)
applies_when: shortest path with negative edges (practical speedup over Bellman-Ford)
sparse graphs where most relaxations are unnecessary
competitive programming where SPFA is fast enough in practice
avoid_when: worst case O(V*E) same as Bellman-Ford — can be adversarially slow

complexity.time: O(V*E) worst, O(E) average in practice

patterns.constraints: queue-based Bellman-Ford; only enqueue vertices whose distance improved; degenerate to O(V*E) on adversarial graphs

patterns.name: Floyd-Warshall
patterns.constraints: triple nested loop: for k, for i, for j

patterns.name: Tarjan's SCC
applies_when: find strongly connected components in directed graph
avoid_when: undirected graph (use regular DFS for components)
only need connectivity, not strong connectivity
patterns.constraints: maintain discovery time and low-link values

patterns.name: Kruskal's MST
applies_when: edges are naturally given as list
patterns.constraints: sort edges by weight; use union-find

patterns.name: Prim's MST
applies_when: minimum spanning tree on dense graph
avoid_when: edges given as list (Kruskal's is more natural)
patterns.constraints: use priority queue for efficient minimum edge selection

patterns.name: Bipartite Check (2-Coloring)
patterns.constraints: color nodes alternately; conflict = not bipartite

patterns.name: Hungarian Algorithm (Kuhn-Munkres)
applies_when: assign N items from group A to N items in group B with costs
each item assigned to exactly one other (1-to-1)
maximize total value or minimize total cost of assignments
greedy highest-value-first fails because it blocks better global assignments
patterns.constraints: works on bipartite graphs; build cost matrix N×M; for max-weight, negate costs and run min-cost version

patterns.name: Bipartite Matching (Hopcroft-Karp)
applies_when: find maximum cardinality matching in bipartite graph
binary search on answer + matching feasibility check
avoid_when: simple greedy matching suffices (e.g., all items compatible)
patterns.constraints: Hopcroft-Karp for unweighted; for weighted use Hungarian or min-cost-max-flow

patterns.name: Min-Cost Max-Flow
applies_when: assignment problem where items have capacities > 1
flow network with both capacities and per-unit costs
generalized bipartite matching with weights and capacities
avoid_when: no costs on edges (use max-flow alone)
graph is small enough for Hungarian O(N^3)

complexity.time: O(V^2 * E) or O(V * E * log V) with potentials

patterns.constraints: model as flow network: source → left nodes → right nodes → sink; edge costs = negative revenue for max-revenue problems

patterns.name: Topological Sort + Longest Path (Critical Path)
applies_when: DAG where independent branches execute in parallel
parent waits for ALL children to complete (slowest child is bottleneck)
find total completion time = longest path, not shortest
avoid_when: execution is sequential, not parallel (use regular shortest/longest path)
patterns.constraints: topological sort then DP: for each node, completion_time[v] = processing_time[v] + max(completion_time[u] for all parents u); the answer is max over all nodes

patterns.name: Kosaraju's SCC
applies_when: find all SCCs in directed graph
build condensation DAG for further processing (2-SAT)
avoid_when: Tarjan's single-pass is preferred for efficiency
patterns.constraints: two DFS passes: first on original graph for finish order, second on transposed graph in reverse finish order

patterns.name: DFS Traversal (Generic)
applies_when: cycle detection, topological sort, connected components
pathfinding where any path suffices (not shortest)
avoid_when: need shortest path in unweighted graph (use BFS)
patterns.constraints: uses stack (call stack or explicit); may hit recursion limit for deep graphs

patterns.name: Dinic's Algorithm (Maximum Flow)
applies_when: find maximum flow from source to sink
bipartite matching at scale (O(E*sqrt(V)) on unit networks)
avoid_when: only need simple augmenting paths (Edmonds-Karp is simpler)
graph is too small to justify complexity
patterns.constraints: layered BFS + blocking flow DFS; O(E*sqrt(V)) on unit-capacity networks; consistently fast in practice

patterns.name: Articulation Points (Cut Vertices)
applies_when: find critical nodes whose removal disconnects the graph
avoid_when: need edge criticality not vertex (use bridge finding)
graph is already known to be 2-connected
patterns.constraints: DFS with disc[]/low[] arrays; root is AP if 2+ children; non-root v is AP if child to has low[to] >= disc[v]

patterns.name: Bridge Finding (Cut Edges)
applies_when: find critical edges whose removal disconnects the graph
patterns.constraints: DFS with disc[]/low[]; edge (v,to) is bridge if low[to] > disc[v] (strictly greater, unlike articulation points which use >=)

patterns.name: Eulerian Path/Circuit (Hierholzer's)
applies_when: visit every edge exactly once (Eulerian path/circuit)
avoid_when: degree conditions not satisfied (no Eulerian path exists)
need shortest path not edge-covering path
patterns.constraints: undirected: circuit if all even degree, path if exactly 2 odd; directed: circuit if in-deg=out-deg for all, path if one vertex out-in=1 and one in-out=1

patterns.name: 2-SAT (Boolean Satisfiability)
applies_when: boolean formula with exactly 2 literals per clause
scheduling with mutual exclusions or forced implications
avoid_when: need to count solutions or find optimal
patterns.constraints: convert (a OR b) to implications NOT a->b and NOT b->a; build implication graph; find SCCs; unsatisfiable iff x and NOT x in same SCC

patterns.name: Functional Graph (Successor Graph)
applies_when: each state has unique successor (f: V -> V)
find where you end up after K steps (binary lifting)
avoid_when: nodes have multiple outgoing edges (general graph)

complexity.time: O(N log N) build, O(log N) per query

patterns.constraints: binary lifting table: jump[v][k] = 2^k-th successor of v; cycle detection via Floyd's or coloring in O(N)

patterns.name: Geometric Sweep Line
applies_when: closest pair via sweep with active set

complexity.time: O(N log N) typical, O((N+K) log N) for K intersections

patterns.constraints: sweep vertical line left to right; maintain active set in balanced BST ordered by y-coordinate

patterns.name: Dynamic Connectivity
applies_when: edges arrive one at a time
need to answer 'are x and y connected?' dynamically
avoid_when: edges can be removed (need link-cut tree)
patterns.constraints: path compression + union by rank

patterns.name: Cycle Detection via Union-Find
avoid_when: need to find the actual cycle path
patterns.constraints: if find(u) == find(v) before union, cycle exists

patterns.name: Bloom Pre-Filter
applies_when: need to quickly reject non-members before expensive check
memory is constrained and false positives are acceptable
large set with infrequent positive lookups
avoid_when: need deletion support (use counting bloom filter)
set is small enough for exact membership
patterns.constraints: k = number of hash functions; m = bit array size; tune for target FP rate

patterns.name: Skip List Ordered Operations
applies_when: need ordered set/map with concurrent access
avoid_when: space is critical (overhead of multiple levels)
patterns.constraints: coin-flip level assignment; p = 0.5 or 0.25

patterns.name: B-Tree Disk-Optimized Storage
avoid_when: data fits in memory (use balanced BST or hashmap)
patterns.constraints: B chosen to match disk page size (typically 4KB-16KB)

patterns.name: LSM Tree (Log-Structured Merge)
applies_when: used in LevelDB, RocksDB, Cassandra, HBase

complexity.time: O(1) amortized write, O(L) read where L=levels

patterns.constraints: write amplification 10-30x; Bloom filters on SSTables reduce read cost; leveled vs tiered compaction tradeoff

patterns.name: Streaming Data Buffer
applies_when: moving average or recent history window
avoid_when: need random access by content (use hashmap)
patterns.constraints: k = buffer capacity; head and tail pointers wrap around

patterns.name: Suffix Array Substring Search
applies_when: many substring queries on same text

complexity.time: O(n log n) build, O(m log n) query

patterns.constraints: pair with LCP array for repeated substring problems

patterns.name: Suffix Array (Pattern Alternative)
applies_when: multiple substring searches on fixed text
text is fixed and patterns vary

complexity.time: O(n log n) build, O(m log n) per query

patterns.constraints: sort all suffixes; binary search for pattern; LCP array enables O(1) LCP queries with RMQ

patterns.name: Memoization (Top-Down)
applies_when: not all subproblems need to be solved
avoid_when: all subproblems will be solved (tabulation avoids overhead)
recursion depth would cause stack overflow
space optimization by rolling array is needed
patterns.constraints: cache key must fully represent state

patterns.name: Tabulation (Bottom-Up)
applies_when: all subproblems need to be solved
space optimization by keeping only last row/column
patterns.constraints: determine correct fill order from base cases

patterns.name: Bitmask DP
patterns.constraints: n <= 20 typically; use bit operations for set manipulation

patterns.name: 0/1 Knapsack
applies_when: each item used at most once
avoid_when: items can be used unlimited times (unbounded knapsack)
patterns.constraints: W = capacity; optimize space with 1D array iterating backwards

patterns.name: Longest Common Subsequence
applies_when: find longest common subsequence of two sequences
minimum edit to make sequences equal
avoid_when: need contiguous match (use longest common substring)
sequences are very long and similar (specialized algorithms exist)
patterns.constraints: if chars match: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1; else max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1])

patterns.name: Edit Distance (Levenshtein)
applies_when: minimum edits to transform one string to another
avoid_when: only need to check if edit distance <= k (optimized approaches exist)
strings are very long (O(nm) may be too slow)
patterns.constraints: three operations: insert, delete, replace

patterns.name: Interval DP
applies_when: optimal strategy for merging/splitting contiguous ranges
patterns.constraints: iterate by interval length, then start position, then split point

patterns.name: Digit DP
applies_when: count numbers in [L, R] satisfying digit properties
avoid_when: range is small enough to enumerate
patterns.constraints: track: position, tight flag, and problem-specific state

patterns.name: DP on Trees
applies_when: max path sum, tree diameter, house robber III
avoid_when: tree is actually a graph with cycles
problem does not decompose by subtrees
patterns.constraints: postorder traversal: solve children before parent

patterns.name: State Machine DP
applies_when: problem has well-defined states and transitions
stock trading with constraints (cooldown, fee, k transactions)
patterns.constraints: define states clearly; optimize space since only previous step needed

patterns.name: Longest Increasing Subsequence
applies_when: find length of longest increasing subsequence
patterns.constraints: maintain tails array with binary search for O(n log n)

patterns.name: Grouped/Bundled Knapsack
applies_when: items belong to groups/bundles where taking one means taking all
each group has a combined weight and combined value (possibly with bonus)
individual items can be taken freely, but grouped items are all-or-nothing
standard 0/1 knapsack after collapsing each group into a single super-item
avoid_when: groups allow partial selection (use group knapsack with at-most-one-per-group)
N and W are too large for pseudo-polynomial DP

complexity.time: O(G*W) where G = number of groups + individuals

patterns.constraints: precompute each group's total weight and total value (with any bonuses); then run standard 0/1 knapsack on collapsed items

patterns.name: Weighted Interval Scheduling DP
applies_when: select non-overlapping intervals to maximize total profit/weight
each interval has a value (not all equal)
greedy sort-by-end fails because intervals have different weights
avoid_when: all intervals have equal weight (use greedy sort-by-end)
patterns.constraints: sort by end time; for each interval, binary search for latest non-overlapping predecessor; dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], value[i] + dp[p(i)])

patterns.name: Unbounded Knapsack
applies_when: items can be used multiple times (coin change, rod cutting)
capacity W is small enough for DP
avoid_when: items used at most once (use 0/1 knapsack)
patterns.constraints: 1D DP: dp[w] = max over all items i of (dp[w - weight[i]] + value[i]); iterate capacity left to right (unlike 0/1 which goes right to left)

patterns.name: Greedy Fractional Knapsack
patterns.constraints: sort by value/weight ratio descending; take greedily; can be solved in O(n) via median-of-medians but rarely implemented

patterns.name: DP O(n^2) (Classic LIS / Pairwise DP)
applies_when: need to reconstruct actual subsequence (easier with DP array)
variants where O(n log n) optimization doesn't apply
avoid_when: n > 5000-10000 (use O(n log n) with binary search)
only length needed on large n
patterns.constraints: dp[i] = best value ending at index i; for each i, check all j < i

patterns.name: Brute Force Enumeration
applies_when: need to verify correctness of optimized solution
patterns.constraints: baseline correctness check; generate all candidates, test each

patterns.name: Divide and Conquer DP Optimization
applies_when: DP recurrence dp[i][j] = min over k {dp[i-1][k-1] + C(k,j)}
optimal split points are monotone non-decreasing
avoid_when: optimal split points are not monotone
patterns.constraints: divide and conquer on the answer range; compute dp[i][mid] then recurse on left/right with narrowed opt range

patterns.name: Knuth's DP Optimization
applies_when: interval DP: dp[i][j] = min over k {dp[i][k] + dp[k+1][j] + C(i,j)}
cost satisfies monotonicity and quadrilateral inequality
optimal BST, matrix chain-like, cutting problems
avoid_when: recurrence is not interval DP shape
patterns.constraints: key insight: opt(i,j-1) <= opt(i,j) <= opt(i+1,j); iterate diagonals with narrowed search

patterns.name: Slope Trick
applies_when: DP values form piecewise-linear convex function
transitions add |x-c| or take prefix/suffix minima
make array non-decreasing with minimum cost
avoid_when: DP value function is not convex/piecewise-linear
transitions can't be expressed as slope changes
N <= 5000 where O(N^2) naive DP is fine
patterns.constraints: represent convex function by its slope change points in a priority queue; transitions modify the queue

patterns.name: Persistent Segment Tree
applies_when: need access to multiple versions of data structure simultaneously
offline versioning: query as if only first i updates happened
avoid_when: only current version matters (plain segment tree)
memory tight (each update costs O(log N) extra nodes)
range updates with lazy propagation (complex on persistent trees)
patterns.constraints: path copying: each update creates O(log N) new nodes, shares rest with previous version

patterns.name: Subsets / Power Set
avoid_when: only need count, not enumeration (use math/DP)
patterns.constraints: handle duplicates by sorting and skipping

patterns.name: Permutations
avoid_when: only need next permutation (use iterative algorithm)
patterns.constraints: sort input and skip duplicates for unique permutations

patterns.name: Combinations
applies_when: combination sum with reuse or no-reuse
patterns.constraints: use start index to avoid generating same combination

patterns.name: Constraint Satisfaction Backtracking
avoid_when: constraints can be checked globally (use math/greedy)
patterns.constraints: prune early: check constraints before recursing

patterns.name: Divide and Conquer
applies_when: subproblems do not overlap (otherwise use DP)
patterns.constraints: use Master Theorem to analyze recurrence

patterns.name: Backtracking (Generic)
applies_when: problem has choices that can be undone
avoid_when: no useful pruning possible (bitmask DP may be better)
patterns.constraints: effectiveness depends on quality of pruning; always check constraints before recursing deeper

patterns.name: Next Permutation (Narayana Pandita's)
avoid_when: order doesn't matter (Heap's algorithm has fewer swaps)
patterns.constraints: find rightmost ascent, swap with smallest larger element to right, reverse suffix; Narayana Pandita (14th century)

patterns.name: Sprague-Grundy Theorem / Nim
applies_when: two-player impartial game with perfect information
games decomposable into independent sub-games (XOR Grundy values)
avoid_when: partisan games (chess, Go) where players have different moves
games with randomness or hidden information
state space too large to enumerate Grundy values
patterns.constraints: Grundy number g(s) = mex({g(s') : s' reachable}); position is losing iff g=0; combined game: XOR individual Grundy numbers; Nim: XOR pile sizes
